portfolio_rationale: GOOGL stands out as the highest-conviction pick with a clean technical sweep (all momentum indicators bullish, RSI not overbought, volume surge, +8.4% 5-day return) combined with strong fundamentals and no bear-case technical evidence — the bear case relied entirely on macro headwinds that apply equally to all names. LLY earns second place on exceptional revenue growth (54% YoY), best-in-class margins, and strong price momentum, though overbought RSI (89) and leverage risk warrant a discount. AAPL and XOM are solid but less differentiated; AMZN's deeply oversold technicals (RSI 19, price well below 20-day MA) introduce near-term uncertainty despite strong fundamentals, and BRK.B's mixed signal history and overbought RSI with the weakest bull-case evidence balance make it the lowest-conviction hold.

GOOGL.reason: Strongest technical picture in the universe with all momentum indicators aligned bullish, volume confirmation, and solid fundamentals with minimal unaddressed bear-case evidence.

LLY.reason: Best fundamental growth profile (54% revenue growth, 83% gross margin) with positive price momentum, partially offset by extreme RSI overbought reading and elevated leverage.

AAPL.reason: Consistent bullish signal across three weeks with strong profitability and cash flow; minor liquidity and momentum concerns are well-rebutted.

XOM.reason: Clean technical setup with all moving averages aligned and positive MACD, supported by positive free cash flow, though thin fundamental disclosure and macro headwinds limit conviction.

AMZN.reason: Strong fundamentals and oversold RSI (19) offer rebound potential, but price trading well below 20-day MA with broad momentum deterioration warrants a reduced position.

BRK.B.reason: Weakest bull-case evidence quality with overbought RSI, declining search interest, mixed signal history including a recent bearish week, and the fewest fundamental data points available.
